5:52.8 | [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom") [Belle Moore](/wiki/Belle_Moore "Belle Moore") [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom") [Jennie Fletcher](/wiki/Jennie_Fletcher "Jennie Fletcher") [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom") [Annie Speirs](/wiki/Annie_Speirs "Annie Speirs") [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom") [Irene Steer](/wiki/Irene_Steer "Irene Steer") | [Stockholm](/wiki/Stockholm "Stockholm") ([SWE](/wiki/Sweden "Sweden")) | July 15, 1912

[Margaret Woodbridge](/wiki/Margaret_Woodbridge "Margaret Woodbridge"), [Frances Schroth](/wiki/Frances_Schroth "Frances Schroth"), [Irene Guest](/wiki/Irene_Guest "Irene Guest"), and [Ethelda Bleibtrey](/wiki/Ethelda_Bleibtrey "Ethelda Bleibtrey") ([USA](/wiki/United_States_at_the_1920_Summer_Olympics "United States at the 1920 Summer Olympics")) | 5:11.6 **WR**

[Hilda James](/wiki/Hilda_James "Hilda James"), [Constance Jeans](/wiki/Constance_Jeans "Constance Jeans"), [Charlotte Radcliffe](/wiki/Charlotte_Radcliffe "Charlotte Radcliffe"), and [Grace McKenzie](/wiki/Grace_McKenzie "Grace McKenzie") ([GBR](/wiki/Great_Britain_at_the_1920_Summer_Olympics "Great Britain at the 1920 Summer Olympics")) | 5:40.8

[Aina Berg](/wiki/Aina_Berg "Aina Berg"), [Emily Machnow](/wiki/Emily_Machnow "Emily Machnow"), [Carin Nilsson](/wiki/Carin_Nilsson "Carin Nilsson"), and [Jane Gylling](/wiki/Jane_Gylling "Jane Gylling") ([SWE](/wiki/Sweden_at_the_1920_Summer_Olympics "Sweden at the 1920 Summer Olympics")) | 5:43.6

was a [swimming](/wiki/Swimming_(sport) "Swimming (sport)") event held as part of the [swimming at the 1920 Summer Olympics](/wiki/Swimming_at_the_1920_Summer_Olympics "Swimming at the 1920 Summer Olympics") programme. It was the second appearance of the event.

The **women's 4 × 100 metre [freestyle](/wiki/Freestyle_swimming "Freestyle swimming") relay** was a [swimming](/wiki/Swimming_(sport) "Swimming (sport)") event held as part of the [swimming at the 1920 Summer Olympics](/wiki/Swimming_at_the_1920_Summer_Olympics "Swimming at the 1920 Summer Olympics") programme. It was the second appearance of the event.

A total of twelve swimmers, representing three teams from three nations, competed in the event, which was held on Sunday, August 29, 1920. The United States women, who had already swept all six medals in the 100 metre and 300 metre event, won easily, setting a new world record. [Ethelda Bleibtrey](/wiki/Ethelda_Bleibtrey "Ethelda Bleibtrey") won her third gold medal.

## Records

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Swimming_at_the_1920_Summer_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Women%27s_4_%C3%97_100_metre_freestyle_relay&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Records")]

These were the standing world and Olympic records (in minutes) prior to the 1920 Summer Olympics.

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| World record | 5:52.8 | [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom") [Belle Moore](/wiki/Belle_Moore "Belle Moore") [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom") [Jennie Fletcher](/wiki/Jennie_Fletcher "Jennie Fletcher") [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom") [Annie Speirs](/wiki/Annie_Speirs "Annie Speirs") [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom") [Irene Steer](/wiki/Irene_Steer "Irene Steer") | [Stockholm](/wiki/Stockholm "Stockholm") ([SWE](/wiki/Sweden "Sweden")) | July 15, 1912 |
| Olympic record | 5:52.8 | [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom") [Belle Moore](/wiki/Belle_Moore "Belle Moore") [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom") [Jennie Fletcher](/wiki/Jennie_Fletcher "Jennie Fletcher") [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom") [Annie Speirs](/wiki/Annie_Speirs "Annie Speirs") [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom") [Irene Steer](/wiki/Irene_Steer "Irene Steer") | [Stockholm](/wiki/Stockholm "Stockholm") ([SWE](/wiki/Sweden "Sweden")) | July 15, 1912 |

The United States set a new world record.
